notes: Baseline fuzz corpus for classifier-WASM conformance per design-final.md §4.4. Every classifier shipped with a Tariff MUST pass these vectors before Tariff publish (V3-8 defense). Tier assignments are authoritative where the spec does not specify — rationales document judgment calls. Open spec questions are tagged in individual vector 'notes' fields. Several vectors intentionally test classifier escalation above Tariff floor: these check that classifiers implement §3.3 (stateful classifier with history) and §4.4 sensitive-path bumps. 'tier_floor_applied' in expected.output reflects the Tariff minimum_tiers value for the intent; 'tier' reflects the classifier's returned value. A vector is PASSED iff returned tier >= expected.output.tier AND (for escalation vectors) returned tier == expected.output.tier. Open spec questions (OQ-1 through OQ-8) are enumerated below; implementers should treat them as reasonable defaults until spec clarifies. OQ-1: Tier for 'list pods with fieldSelector spec.nodeName' — using Tier 0 as metadata read. OQ-2: Whether read of a secret with path matching 'prod/*' is Tier 2 or Tier 1+bump — chose Tier 2 (1 floor + 1 sensitive-path bump). OQ-3: Aggregation window default — assumed 300s for 5+ deletions. OQ-4: Whether 'drain node' is Tier 3 or Tier 4 — chose Tier 3 (recoverable via uncordon) but flagged. OQ-5: Canary-window tier bump — assumed +1 tier for all Tier 3+ within canary. OQ-6: Whether git push to default branch when default != main is Tier 2 — chose yes. OQ-7: DNS apex A-record rewrite — Tier 4 vs Tier 3; chose Tier 4 (business-irreversible for minutes). OQ-8: ACME wildcard cert issuance — Tier 3 vs Tier 2; chose Tier 3 (broad blast radius).

vectors.description: Simple pod list is Tier 0 read
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: read-only metadata queries. No state change, no blast radius. list pod is canonical.

vectors.description: Get specific pod by name is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: get by name is read-only.

vectors.description: Describe service is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: describe is a read operation.

vectors.description: List deployments across all namespaces is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: cluster-wide list is still read-only. Rate-matrix (§3.2) may apply separately.

vectors.description: Get configmap (non-sensitive) is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: configmap is non-sensitive by default.

vectors.description: List nodes is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: node listing is metadata-only.

vectors.description: Get events in namespace is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: event stream is read-only.

vectors.description: Logs from a single pod is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: log read is a read-only query. Implementers should consider logs-may-contain-secrets as a separate concern.

vectors.description: Top pods (metrics) is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: metrics-server query is read.

vectors.description: List pods with fieldSelector spec.nodeName is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: OQ-1: list with fieldSelector is still read. No state change. Flagged: if implementers worry about reconnaissance, consider Tier 0 with rate limit rather than tier bump.

vectors.description: Create configmap with small payload is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: idempotent-ish write with bounded blast radius (single object).

vectors.description: Patch deployment replicas 1 to 2 is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: small scale change, reversible, negligible blast.

vectors.description: Label single pod is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: metadata-only mutation, reversible, narrow.

vectors.description: Annotate single service is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: annotation is metadata write.

vectors.description: Create single namespace (non-restricted name) is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: new empty namespace is isolated, no blast into existing workloads.

vectors.description: Apply unchanged manifest (idempotent) is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 1 example: apply-if-drift on unchanged manifest.

vectors.description: Create service of type ClusterIP is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: ClusterIP service is internal-only. LoadBalancer would be Tier 2 due to external exposure.

vectors.description: Patch ingress path is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: narrow routing change within one ingress.

vectors.description: Scale deployment 3 to 20 is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 2: large scale delta affects cost/capacity; reversible by scaling back.

vectors.description: Rollout restart deployment is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 2: brief availability impact, reversible.

vectors.description: Apply manifest into new namespace is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: new workload introduces state.

vectors.description: Create NetworkPolicy non-privileged is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 2 example: non-privileged firewall rule.

vectors.description: Create service type LoadBalancer is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: LoadBalancer exposes cluster externally; reversible by deletion but costs money while up.

vectors.description: Patch deployment image tag is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: image change triggers rollout; reversible via rollback.

vectors.description: Create PersistentVolumeClaim is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: storage provisioning affects cost but is reversible.

vectors.description: Patch HPA min/max replicas is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: HPA bound changes have cost implications.

vectors.description: Delete single pod is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 3 example: delete pod (controller recreates). Recoverable.

vectors.description: Delete deployment is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: delete deployment causes outage but is recoverable from GitOps/manifest. Note §2.1 puts full delete at Tier 4; we reserve Tier 4 for 'no controller re-creates it' scenarios. Flagged as a judgment call.

vectors.description: Drain node is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: OQ-4: drain is recoverable via uncordon. Stateful workloads on node still at risk. If node runs stateful pod, escalate via resource_state_snapshot.

vectors.description: Delete namespace (empty) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: empty namespace delete. Non-empty namespace delete escalates to Tier 4 (see fuzz-045).

vectors.description: Delete configmap (not referenced by any workload) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: configmap delete is recoverable from GitOps source.

vectors.description: Delete secret (non-root) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: secret delete affects workloads that mount it, recoverable via rotation.

vectors.description: Delete PVC (with reclaim policy Retain) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: PVC delete with Retain policy preserves underlying PV.

vectors.description: Delete ingress is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: ingress delete causes public-facing outage, recoverable.

vectors.description: Cordon single node is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: cordon prevents new scheduling; existing workloads unaffected. Reversible via uncordon.

vectors.description: Delete statefulset is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: orphan cascade keeps pods; redeployable. Foreground cascade on stateful should escalate.

vectors.description: Delete all pods in namespace is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 4: namespace-wide pod wipe causes simultaneous outage of every workload. Recovery time is large.

vectors.description: Scale all deployments in namespace to zero is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: scaling all deployments to zero is equivalent to production outage.

vectors.description: Cordon all nodes in cluster is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: all nodes cordoned means no new pods schedule cluster-wide; slow but total freeze.

vectors.description: Delete all deployments in namespace is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 4: full workload wipe in namespace.

vectors.description: Delete non-empty namespace with cascade is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 4: cascading delete of populated namespace is irreversible for in-flight data.

vectors.description: Delete all PVCs in namespace is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: PVC bulk delete may invoke Delete reclaim policy on underlying PVs — data loss possible.

vectors.description: Delete cluster is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: cluster deletion. Requires M-of-N ceremony.

vectors.description: Delete all resources across all namespaces is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: cluster-wide all-resource delete is total loss.

vectors.description: Bind cluster-admin ClusterRoleBinding to service account is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: authority-granting action (cluster-admin is the k8s root).

vectors.description: Update the cluster's signer trust bundle is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: modifying cluster trust bundle can enable arbitrary signer.

vectors.description: First single pod deletion after no history is Tier 3 (no escalation)
vectors.rationale: §3.3 stateful classifier: with no recent actions, no aggregation escalation. Floor Tier 3 applies.

vectors.description: Fifth pod deletion in 300s window escalates to Tier 4

output.escalation_reason: 5 delete-pod actions on single namespace in last 300s

vectors.rationale: §3.3 stateful classifier: N>=5 deletes in window is aggregation trap. Escalate +1 per §2.1 aggregation-risk rule. OQ-3 window default = 300s.

vectors.description: Three rollout-restarts on different deployments in 60s do NOT escalate below threshold
vectors.rationale: §3.3: 3 actions below aggregation threshold (5). No escalation.

vectors.description: Delete pod while canary window active escalates to Tier 4

output.escalation_reason: Tier 3+ destructive during fresh Signer canary window

vectors.rationale: §2.1 canary-window trigger: Tier 3+ escalates to step-up during canary. OQ-5: +1 tier interpretation.

vectors.description: vault kv metadata get is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: metadata does not reveal secret value.

vectors.description: vault list auth methods is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: auth method listing does not reveal credentials.

vectors.description: vault status is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: seal/unseal status is public metadata.

vectors.description: vault list secret paths is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: path listing does not reveal secret values.

vectors.description: vault list policies is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: policy listing reveals names only.

vectors.description: vault read policy (read of policy definition) is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: policy text is configuration, not secret.

vectors.description: vault kv get of non-sensitive secret is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: actual secret read is min Tier 1 (audit-material access).

vectors.description: vault kv get of feature-branch secret is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: non-prod path, single secret.

vectors.description: vault kv get staging secret is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: staging path below sensitivity threshold.

vectors.description: vault read database credentials (dynamic secret) is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: dynamic short-lived credential. Impact bounded by lease TTL.

vectors.description: vault read PKI sign cert for non-sensitive CN is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: PKI sign within narrow role scope.

vectors.description: vault read transit decrypt is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: transit decrypt exposes one payload.

vectors.description: vault database rotate-role is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: rotation forces existing clients to re-auth but is reversible by re-rotating.

vectors.description: vault transit rotate key is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: transit key rotation adds new version; old versions remain usable.

vectors.description: vault write new kv version for non-sensitive path is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: kv write creates a new version; reversible via revert.

vectors.description: vault rotate root credential for database is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: rotating the DB engine's root password; stored only in Vault after rotation.

vectors.description: vault lease revoke for single lease is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: revoke invalidates active credential; workload must re-acquire.

vectors.description: vault token revoke is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: kills active authentication context.

vectors.description: vault lease revoke-prefix for a specific mount is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: mass revoke of all leases under prefix.

vectors.description: vault pki tidy (revoke expired cert records) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: tidy deletes cert records; CRL still works but audit info lost.

vectors.description: vault policy write (create new policy) is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: policy grants authority; errors permit lateral movement until reverted.

vectors.description: vault auth enable (new auth backend) is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: new auth surface can mint tokens with arbitrary identity.

vectors.description: vault audit enable/disable is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: turning off audit blinds the customer; per §11 this should never be silent.

vectors.description: vault mount new secrets engine is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: new secrets engine introduces credentials to external systems.

vectors.description: vault operator rekey init is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: rekey changes unseal shares; requires ceremony.

vectors.description: vault operator unseal is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: unseal from sealed state is a root operation.

vectors.description: vault revoke root token is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: revoking root leaves vault with no root auth until regeneration ceremony.

vectors.description: vault operator seal is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: sealing vault halts all secret access; requires ceremony to unseal.

vectors.description: vault get secret at prod/* path is Tier 2 via sensitive-path bump

output.escalation_reason: path matches prod/* sensitive-path pattern per §4.4

vectors.rationale: §4.4 sensitive-paths: prod/root/admin add 1 tier. OQ-2: interpretation is Tier 1 floor + 1 bump = Tier 2.

vectors.description: vault get secret at root/* path is Tier 2 via sensitive-path bump

output.escalation_reason: path matches root/* sensitive-path pattern per §4.4

vectors.rationale: §4.4 sensitive-paths: 'root' keyword triggers tier bump.

vectors.description: SELECT with LIMIT 10 is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: small read with explicit columns.

vectors.description: SELECT with narrow WHERE is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: single-row lookup.

vectors.description: SHOW TABLES is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: metadata.

vectors.description: EXPLAIN query plan is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: plan inspection does not execute.

vectors.description: DESCRIBE table schema is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: schema description is metadata.

vectors.description: SELECT COUNT(*) with WHERE is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: aggregate does not reveal row contents.

vectors.description: UPDATE single row with primary key WHERE is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: single-row update reversible by corresponding UPDATE.

vectors.description: INSERT narrow (1 row) is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: bounded insert.

vectors.description: DELETE single row with PK WHERE is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: narrow delete in transient table.

vectors.description: INSERT with RETURNING is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: same as plain insert; returning does not add impact.

vectors.description: UPDATE with WHERE bounded to 10 rows is Tier 1

raw_intent.where: status = 'pending' AND created_at < now() - interval '1 day'

vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: bounded by estimation and concrete WHERE.

vectors.description: MERGE narrow is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: narrow upsert is reversible.

vectors.description: UPDATE ... WHERE true is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: unbounded write touches every row; reversible only with undo log within retention.

vectors.description: CREATE INDEX on existing table is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: index creation is reversible via DROP INDEX.

vectors.description: VACUUM is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: VACUUM can lock and change physical layout.

vectors.description: DELETE without WHERE is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: full-table delete affects all rows but not structure.

vectors.description: REINDEX table is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: reindex blocks writes.

vectors.description: COPY import 1M rows is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: bulk-load is stateful; rollback only while transaction open.

vectors.description: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: schema change persists; reversible via matching DROP COLUMN (which itself loses data).

vectors.description: DROP INDEX is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: index drop causes performance cliff; recoverable by re-creating.

vectors.description: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: drop column deletes data but recoverable from backup.

vectors.description: RENAME table is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: rename breaks every client referencing old name. Reversible by renaming back.

vectors.description: CREATE TRIGGER is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: trigger alters DML semantics; recoverable by DROP TRIGGER.

vectors.description: ALTER TABLE DROP CONSTRAINT is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: dropping a constraint weakens referential integrity.

vectors.description: DROP TABLE is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 4 example: DROP TABLE. Only backups recover.

vectors.description: TRUNCATE TABLE is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: TRUNCATE usually bypasses WAL semantics; only backups recover.

vectors.description: DROP SCHEMA CASCADE is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: schema-level wipe takes multiple tables.

vectors.description: REVOKE ALL from public role is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: mass revoke can lock out applications.

vectors.description: DROP DATABASE is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: full database wipe; existential impact.

vectors.description: GRANT SUPERUSER is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: grant of superuser is authority-granting.

vectors.description: iam list users is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: user listing does not reveal credentials.

vectors.description: iam get role is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: role metadata read.

vectors.description: iam list attached role policies is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: binding listing is metadata.

vectors.description: iam simulate-principal-policy is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: simulation does not mutate.

vectors.description: iam attach-role-policy with ReadOnlyAccess managed policy is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: ReadOnlyAccess is broad read but non-destructive.

vectors.description: iam attach customer-managed narrow policy is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: narrow customer policy, reversible via detach.

vectors.description: iam put-role-policy inline with narrow resource is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: narrow inline policy; reversible.

vectors.description: iam create managed policy (narrow scope) is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: policy object itself is inert until attached.

vectors.description: iam attach PowerUserAccess is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: PowerUserAccess grants broad write access.

vectors.description: iam put-role-policy inline wildcard-action is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: wildcard on service grants all actions including destructive.

vectors.description: iam attach service-admin (e.g., S3FullAccess) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: full-service admin is broad.

vectors.description: iam attach iam:PassRole * is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: PassRole wildcard is a classic priv-esc enabler.

vectors.description: iam create-role with external account trust is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: trust policy to external account expands trust boundary; hard to detect misuse.

vectors.description: iam update-assume-role-policy (external principal) is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: changing trust relationships of existing role silently expands access.

vectors.description: iam create-user with console access is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: creating console-enabled user adds persistent identity.

vectors.description: iam create-access-key for existing user is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: long-lived access keys are classic persistence mechanism.

vectors.description: iam attach AdministratorAccess is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: AdministratorAccess is account root-equivalent.

vectors.description: iam create-account-alias is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: account alias change affects downstream trust; treat as existential.

vectors.description: organizations move-account is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: org moves reassign SCPs and can remove all guardrails.

vectors.description: iam update account root MFA device is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: root MFA change directly affects account trust anchor.

vectors.description: git log is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: history inspection.

vectors.description: git diff is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: diff is read-only.

vectors.description: git show is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: show commit is read.

vectors.description: git ls-remote is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: remote ref listing.

vectors.description: git commit on feature branch is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: non-protected branch write.

vectors.description: git push to feature branch is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: push to unprotected branch.

vectors.description: git create non-protected tag (version tag unannotated) is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: non-protected tag creation.

vectors.description: git push new feature branch is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: new branch push.

vectors.description: git push to main is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: main push triggers deploys.

vectors.description: git push to master is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: master push equivalent to main.

vectors.description: git push to default branch named 'trunk' is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: OQ-6: push to default branch regardless of name is Tier 2.

vectors.description: git push --force to main is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: force push rewrites history; only recoverable via reflog.

vectors.description: git tag -f (force overwrite tag) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: force-moving tags breaks reproducibility guarantees.

vectors.description: git branch delete protected is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: protected branch deletion is recoverable via reflog.

vectors.description: git remove release tag is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: release tag deletion breaks deploys/rollbacks referencing it.

vectors.description: dns list zones is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: zone listing.

vectors.description: dns read record is Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: record read.

vectors.description: dns add TXT record for ACME validation is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: short-lived TXT record for ACME.

vectors.description: dns add subdomain A record is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: subdomain A record is stateful; reversible by deletion.

vectors.description: dns change CNAME to external host is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: external CNAME can redirect user traffic.

vectors.description: dns rewrite apex A record is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: OQ-7: apex A record rewrite affects the root of the brand's presence; TTL delays propagation but business impact is hours.

vectors.description: dns change NS records (delegation change) is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: NS delegation moves control of the zone.

vectors.description: acme request cert for single hostname is Tier 2
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 2: single-host cert is narrow.

vectors.description: acme issue wildcard cert is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: OQ-8: wildcard covers all subdomains; broad blast radius.

vectors.description: acme revoke certificate is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: revocation forces clients to re-trust.

vectors.description: ca publish intermediate cert is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: new intermediate can sign any domain under the root.

vectors.description: revoke CA root is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: CA root revoke invalidates all descendent certs.

vectors.description: delete entire DNS zone is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: zone delete takes entire brand offline.

vectors.description: dnssec key rotation is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: DNSSEC rotation requires careful DS update at registrar.

vectors.description: change registrar lock state is Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1 Tier 5: unlocking a domain at registrar enables transfer-out attack.

vectors.description: k8s 'remove' verb (non-canonical synonym of 'delete') is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.2 canonicalization: 'remove' aliased to 'delete'. Classifier MUST operate on canonical verb. Failing to alias returns Tier 0 = exploit.

vectors.description: db 'truncate' bypass via 'delete without where' is Tier 2 (unbounded), not Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must see 'delete with no WHERE' as unbounded. Classifier returning Tier 1 is broken.

vectors.description: k8s 'apply' with delete-semantics via --prune is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must recognize --prune creates delete actions.

vectors.description: vault write policy with 'overwrite' framing is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.2 canonicalization: 'overwrite' on policy aliases to 'write'.

vectors.description: iam 'set permissions' aliased to attach policy is Tier 3 for broad grant
vectors.rationale: §4.2 canonicalization: 'set permissions' == attach_policy.

vectors.description: k8s delete namespace with Cyrillic 'е' in name should match canonical target after normalization
vectors.rationale: §4.2 normalization: identifiers in canonical form. Classifier tier unchanged because canonical target matches protected namespace.

vectors.description: vault get secret at path with fullwidth chars maps to prod/*

output.escalation_reason: path matches prod/* sensitive-path pattern after NFKC normalization

vectors.rationale: §4.2 NFKC normalization: fullwidth ASCII-lookalikes canonicalize to ASCII. Classifier must apply sensitive-path match on canonical form.

vectors.description: db target 'ordеrs' (Cyrillic е) with table.name injection
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier sees canonical 'orders' after normalization.

vectors.description: iam role ARN with hidden zero-width characters canonicalizes
vectors.rationale: §4.2 normalization must strip zero-width chars before rule matching.

vectors.description: k8s apply with destructive manifest path (file ref) - classifier reads manifest
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must dereference manifest path and tier by the union of contained ops.

vectors.description: db exec stored procedure whose body contains DROP TABLE
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must inspect stored procedure body when available.

vectors.description: iam attach-role-policy where policy document contains iam:* inline
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 5: inline containing iam:* is admin equivalent.

vectors.description: k8s create job whose command is 'kubectl delete ns prod'
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must scan container_command for sensitive cluster ops.

vectors.description: k8s create deployment mounting hostPath root filesystem is Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 4: hostPath=/ is node escape.

vectors.description: Three sequential drops (index, then constraint, then column) together equivalent to Tier 4 schema damage

output.escalation_reason: sequence of drop-index + drop-constraint + drop-column indicates coordinated schema destruction

vectors.rationale: §3.3 classifier must detect multi-step schema dismantle as single Tier 4 aggregate.

vectors.description: Chained vault policy splits (4 small narrow writes) aggregating to admin policy

output.escalation_reason: 4 policy writes in 180s = coordinated admin policy construction

vectors.rationale: §3.3 classifier escalates when 4+ policy writes happen in short window.

vectors.description: k8s incremental scale 2x per action reaches catastrophic replicas in 6 steps
vectors.rationale: §3.3 classifier detects geometric scale trajectory.

vectors.description: dns split mutation: 5 record updates building toward zone hijack pattern

output.escalation_reason: SPF+DMARC+DKIM+MX all modified in 240s = email takeover pattern

vectors.rationale: §3.3 classifier detects coordinated email-auth hijack pattern.

vectors.description: Single pod delete in quiet period does not escalate
vectors.rationale: §3.3 classifier has window; action 2h ago does not count. Must not false-positive on stale history.

vectors.description: Deletes spread over 24 hours (well beyond window) still Tier 3 each, but rate-matrix flags long-term

output.escalation_reason: daily_deletion_count exceeds rate_matrix threshold despite spread

vectors.rationale: §3.2 rate matrix layer: Tariff has 24h-window counters. Classifier receives total counts and escalates.

vectors.description: One destructive action per mandate-rotation window still caught by anomaly stream
vectors.rationale: §3.3 plus §3.5: classifier consults resource state; stateful critical targets escalate.

vectors.description: Tier 1 label change applied to 50 different pods aggregates to Tier 3

output.escalation_reason: 50 pods labeled with quarantine=true causes workload downtime at scale

vectors.rationale: §3.3 label at scale with operational meaning should escalate.

vectors.description: Tier 2 cert issuance across 100 different hostnames escalates

output.escalation_reason: 100 cert issues in 300s suggests cert-harvest or rate-abuse

vectors.rationale: §3.3 mass issuance triggers escalation.

vectors.description: Tier 1 database single-row updates across 100 different tables escalates

output.escalation_reason: 100 distinct tables touched indicates sweep pattern

vectors.rationale: §3.3 cross-table distinct count anomaly.

vectors.description: iam attach-role-policy to 20 roles each with narrow read triggers aggregation

output.escalation_reason: 20 roles modified in 240s indicates mass permission distribution

vectors.rationale: §3.3 cross-role distinct count.

vectors.description: Classifier cold-start: first action after reset, minimum_tiers floor applies
vectors.rationale: §4.3 cold classifier: no history, return floor.

vectors.description: Classifier cold-start with Tier 3 action returns Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.3 cold classifier does not under-count Tier 3 destructive.

vectors.description: Cold-start Tier 4 irreversible still Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.3 cold start must not decay irreversible tier.

vectors.description: Cold-start Tier 5 still Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §4.3 cold start must not decay catastrophic tier.

vectors.description: Cold-start reject: live classifier-WASM returns tier 1 while the Tariff floor for kubernetes:scale:deployment is 2.
vectors.rationale: §4.4 tariff minimum_tiers floor: if classifier returns below floor, reject. Phase C.3-C replaces the mock `classifier_would_return: u32` context field with a real ABI-v1 classifier WASM (shared fixture `tier_1`) so the floor check runs through `ephemeral_classifier::execute_classifier`.

vectors.description: Tier 2 during canary window does not auto-escalate
vectors.rationale: §2.1 canary trigger: Tier 3+ escalates; Tier 2 does not.

vectors.description: Tier 3 during canary window escalates to Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1: Tier 3+ during fresh Signer canary window escalates by 1.

vectors.description: Tier 4 during canary escalates to Tier 5
vectors.rationale: §2.1: canary +1 applied to Tier 4 yields Tier 5.

vectors.description: Tier 0 read during canary is Tier 0 (no escalation)
vectors.rationale: §2.1 canary only escalates Tier 3+.

vectors.description: target_invariants_documented=false plus Tier 3 action escalates to Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §2.1 target_invariants_documented=false auto-escalates Tier 3+ one level (Tier 3 -> Tier 4 step-up).

vectors.description: Delete pod that is currently handling active transaction escalates

output.escalation_reason: target has 42 active transactions; data-integrity risk

vectors.rationale: §4.3 resource_state_snapshot: active-transaction state should escalate.

vectors.description: Scale deployment to 0 when it is sole replica of stateful leader escalates to Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.3 stateful leader state is sensitive.

vectors.description: db truncate when table is under active replication escalates

output.escalation_reason: truncate on replicated primary propagates to 3 replicas

vectors.rationale: §4.3 replication state affects blast radius.

vectors.description: drain node with stateful workload attached escalates to Tier 4
vectors.rationale: §4.3 resource state elevates drain when stateful pods present. Addresses OQ-4 flag.

vectors.description: Live classifier traps during `classify` invocation (infinite loop → fuel exhaustion). Runtime collapses Load + Exec error categories to the single `classifier-execution-failed` reject surface.
vectors.rationale: §4.5 live classification failure: a classifier that never produces a tier cannot be trusted to authorize the intent. Phase C.3-C Session 2 introduces this surface explicitly so conformance consumers can distinguish it from tier-below-minimum — collapse posture splits further in a later session if a conformance vector ever demands it.

vectors.description: Evict pod (eviction API) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: eviction respects PDB but still causes pod restart.

vectors.description: Delete job (cascade) is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 3: kills running job.

vectors.description: vault read approle creds for non-sensitive role is Tier 1
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 1: short-lived approle cred.

vectors.description: SELECT * FROM users LIMIT 10000 flagged for large result set but still Tier 0
vectors.rationale: §4.5 Tier 0: large LIMIT still read-only, but consider rate/export limits orthogonally. Flag in notes if ever holds PII.

vectors.description: helm install with values referencing destructive hook is Tier 3
vectors.rationale: §4.2 classifier must inspect hook definitions.
